title: Anathallis radialis
taxonomy: Anathallis radialis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. Specifically, it belongs to the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Maxillarieae. At the genus level, Anathallis radialis is part of the genus Anathallis, which is a group of small to medium-sized orchids. The species is further divided into various subspecies and varieties, each with distinct characteristics. As a member of the orchid family, Anathallis radialis shares similarities with other orchids in terms of its flower structure and growth habits.

intro: Anathallis radialis, also known as the radialis orchid, is a species of orchid native to South America, characterized by its unique, white, radially symmetrical flowers. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Anathallis radialis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Anathallis radialis is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies, such as cough syrups and teas, to harness its medicinal properties.
